findings.description: Mock violation: vi.mock('./rating-badge') mocks an internal module instead of a system boundary. Per CLAUDE.md testing standards: 'Never mock your own modules or internal functions.' The test should either test ShopIdentity with the real RatingBadge (integration style) or test them separately without mocking.

Uses raw Tailwind gray classes (text-gray-700, text-gray-300, text-gray-600) instead of semantic tokens defined in DESIGN.md (text-text-primary, text-text-meta). Stars use yellow-400 instead of Star Gold #fcd34d. Should use semantic color tokens for brand consistency.

Math.round(rating) rounds 4.5 to 5 stars, which may slightly mislead users when the displayed text shows '4.5' but 5 filled stars appear. Consider Math.floor(rating) for more conservative star display.
Tests verify implementation (mock.calls props) rather than user-visible behavior. Could be rewritten to test what renders without mocking the child component.
Edge case: !rating returns true for rating = 0, hiding the badge. While 0-star ratings are unlikely, the condition could be more explicit: rating === null || rating === undefined.

Test reads data-filled attribute but never asserts on filledStars.length. Comment says '4.5 rounds to 5 filled stars' but no expectation verifies this claim. Should add: expect(filledStars).toHaveLength(5);
